title: glochidion wrightii
intro: Glochidion wrightii, also known as Wright's Glochidion, is a plant species native to the Philippines and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae and classified as a small tree or shrub. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Glochidion wrightii include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves or roots, which can be used to make teas or applied topically as a poultice for skin conditions.
